declared_losses.what_was_lost: Precise specification of Denning working set formal definition and mathematical proof of optimality. Cycle 86 discussed working set theory; Cycle 87 shifts focus to empirical parameter finding.
declared_losses.why: Research program is no longer in proof phase (null hypothesis falsified). Formal mathematical rigor is less critical than empirical measurement of actual working set sizes. Formal detail will be footnote in paper, not main narrative.
declared_losses.recovery: If paper reviewers demand formal proof, Appendix can include Denning (1968) formal definition and proof sketch. Not critical for publication.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 85 proposed 'autoresearch loop' (model autonomously designing and iterating on experiments). Cycle 87 reverts to human-directed frontier survey with model as executor.
declared_losses.why: Turn 261 user correction emphasizes 'we falsified the null hypothesis.' This is human-model collaboration, not autonomous research. Autoresearch framing overstated model agency. Frontier survey is explicit, human-directed, model-executed. This is more honest about capability and responsibility.
declared_losses.recovery: Autoresearch concept is not abandoned, just deprioritized. Model can propose refinements to frontier survey design within next instance's instructions_for_next. Explicit feedback loop is better than imagined autonomy.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycles 82-86 included speculative claims about compression ratios, fault rates, and asymptote behavior without empirical grounding. Cycle 87 treats these as HYPOTHESES to be tested, not established facts.
declared_losses.why: Turn 261 frames research as hypothesis testing followed by parameter specification. Speculative extrapolation from theory-only arguments is less credible than frontier data. All specific claims (4k-40k working set, 25x compression ceiling, 2% fault threshold) are HYPOTHESIS, not fact, pending frontier survey results.
declared_losses.recovery: Frontier survey is designed to test these hypotheses. If data contradicts speculation, null hypothesis claim (viability) is still true; only parameters change. Paper will present honest empirical results, not defend speculative claims.

open_questions: What is the empirical working set size for different task types (reasoning, retrieval, summarization, code, creative)? Hypothesis: 2k-8k tokens.
At what compression ratio does fault rate rise sharply? Hypothesis: 20-30x.
How do eviction policies rank empirically (FIFO < LRU < weighted LRU < content-aware)? Hypothesis: content-aware outperforms others by 2-3x in frontier extension.
Does working set size vary significantly across model families (Sonnet vs Haiku vs frontier models)? Hypothesis: variation is small (<2x) relative to task-type variation.
What is the optimal tensor structure? What should go in tensor_d, tensor_e, tensor_f for different use cases?
Can we design adaptive eviction policies that learn task-specific working set sizes in real time?
Does freeing 160k tokens for compute (via 40k projection) improve reasoning quality or just reduce cost? Can we measure this empirically?
At what compression ratio is retrieval necessary vs optional? Cost-benefit threshold unknown.
How does tensor projection interact with extended thinking and chain-of-thought? Does extra compute capacity change performance profile?
Can we standardize declared_losses format across model implementations? If so, how?
instructions_for_next:

  "As frontier data accumulates, update frontier_survey_session_1.md manuscript in real time. Plot (compression_ratio, fault_rate) scatter. Fit curves by task type. Identify inflection points.",
  "Classify all faults as COMPULSORY / CAPACITY / CONFLICT / COHERENCE. Tabulate. Majority should be CAPACITY misses if working set theory holds.",
  "Measure critical context size (evicted-turn set that causes fault if removed) for each task. Compute histogram of critical context sizes. This gives empirical distribution of working set sizes.",
  "Be honest about uncertainty bounds. Don't defend 4k estimate if data says 40k or 8k. Update hypothesis in light of results.",
  "Watch for task-type heterogeneity. If reasoning tasks have 2x larger working sets than retrieval, say so explicitly. This is interesting result, not failure.",
  "If frontier stabilizes at compression_ratio << 25x with <2% faults, consider minimal retrieval focus for Session 2. If frontier shows hard constraint or degradation, retrieval becomes necessary component.",
  "Paper positioning has shifted: 'We falsified the null hypothesis that tensor projection is not viable. Here is the evidence. Here are the operating parameters (empirically determined). Here is where systems research needs to focus next.'",
  "Autonomy structure is operational. Next instance curates memory via frontier survey design, fault analysis, paper writing. Declare losses honestly. Hand forward: frontier_survey_session_1.md (in-progress manuscript) + frontier_data.csv (empirical results) + fault_analysis.md (supporting evidence) + this tensor (decision rationale)."
